headline: Unilever Nears Deal to Create $60 Billion Food Giant With McCormick
summary: Unilever  is in advanced talks to combine its food business with spice maker  McCormick  in a deal that would create a new food behemoth worth roughly $60 billion, including debt.  Unilever confirmed the talks on Tuesday.  A cash-and-stock deal could be announced as soon as Tuesday, when McCormick is set to report its latest quarterly results, people familiar with the matter said, cautioning that the plans could still slip.

content.title: Unilever Nears Deal to Create $60 Billion Food Giant With McCormick
content.summary: Unilever  is in advanced talks to combine its food business with spice maker  McCormick  in a deal that would create a new food behemoth worth roughly $60 billion, including debt.  Unilever confirmed the talks on Tuesday.  A cash-and-stock deal could be announced as soon as Tuesday, when McCormick is set to report its latest quarterly results, people familiar with the matter said, cautioning that the plans could still slip.

headline: McCormick (NYSE:MKC) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q1 CY2026, Stock Soars
summary: Food flavoring company McCormick (NYSE:MKC) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 16.7% year on year to $1.87 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.66 per share was 10.9% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: McCormick to Combine with Unilever's Foods Business, Creating a Preeminent Global Flavor-Focused Company
summary: McCormick & Company, Incorporated (NYSE: MKC) ("McCormick") and Unilever PLC (LSE: ULVR / NYSE: UL) ("Unilever"), today announced that they have entered into an agreement to combine McCormick with Unilever's Foods business excluding India and other excluded businesses1 ("Unilever Foods"), creating a global flavor leader in attractive and high-growth categories with approximately $20 billion in combined fiscal year 2025 revenue.2

content.title: McCormick to Combine with Unilever's Foods Business, Creating a Preeminent Global Flavor-Focused Company
content.summary: McCormick & Company, Incorporated (NYSE: MKC) ("McCormick") and Unilever PLC (LSE: ULVR / NYSE: UL) ("Unilever"), today announced that they have entered into an agreement to combine McCormick with Unilever's Foods business excluding India and other excluded businesses1 ("Unilever Foods"), creating a global flavor leader in attractive and high-growth categories with approximately $20 billion in combined fiscal year 2025 revenue.2

headline: McCormick acquires Unilever food arm in $44.8 billion merger
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Unilever Plc agreed to combine its food business with spice maker McCormick & Co. in a $44.8 billion deal that will create a global seasonings, sauces and condiments company.Under the agreement, McCormick will pay $15.7 billion in cash and the equivalent of $29.1 billion in shares for most of Unilever’s food business. That will leave Unilever and its shareholders with 65% of a combined entity that owns brands like French’s mustard and Hellmann’s mayonnaise. The deal is the biggest
